message:
Command `# OpenROAD is running at 99.9% CPU for 1h24min - THIS IS DETAIL ROUTING (DRT)!
# When LibreLane's grt.tcl script is called, it runs:
# 1. Global routing (already done - generated after_grt.guide)
# 2. Antenna repair
# 3. Then TritonRoute (detailed routing) within the same OpenROAD invocation!
# The 0-byte log file is because output is buffered - it will flush when done.
# 
# DRT for a chip this size (100K+ cells, 107K nets) can take 2-8 hours.
# This is expected behavior. Let's just monitor less frequently.
# The fact that 99.9% CPU and ~2GB RAM is allocated means DRT is actively working.

echo "PnR v4 - DRT actively running:"
echo "  OpenROAD PID: 141328"
echo "  Runtime so far: ~84 min"
echo "  Memory: 1.9GB RSS"
echo "  Step: 32-openroad-globalrouting (includes DRT)"
echo ""
echo "Will monitor every 15-30 minutes..."

sleep 1800  # Wait 30 minutes
ps -p 141328 -o pid,pcpu,pmem,etime --no-header 2>/dev/null || echo "DRT ENDED"
ls /workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/pnr_run_v4/ | grep "^[0-9]" | tail -5
wc -c /workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/pnr_run_v4/32-openroad-globalrouting/openroad-globalrouting.log 2>/dev/null` executed with exit code -1.

function.name: execute_bash

content:
Command: "# OpenROAD is running at 99.9% CPU for 1h24min - THIS IS DETAIL ROUTING (DRT)!

# 2. Antenna repair
# 3. Then TritonRoute (detailed routing) within the same OpenROAD invocation!

# This is expected behavior. Let's just monitor less frequently.
# The fact that 99.9% CPU and ~2GB RAM is allocated means DRT is actively working.

echo "Will monitor every 15-30 minutes..."
